  Type: paragraph (informational).
  Context: Explains that built-in default drinks (water, coffee, tea, etc.) cannot be modified; suggests hiding the drink from the list if not needed.

t.ar: لا يُمكن تعديل المشروبات القياسية. ولكن يُمكنك إخفاءها من القائمة إذا كنت لا تحتاج إليها.
t.da: Standarddrikke kan ikke ændres, men du kan skjule dem fra listen, hvis du ikke har brug for dem.
t.de: Standardgetränke können nicht bearbeitet werden. Wenn du ein Getränk nicht brauchst, kannst du es jedoch aus der Liste ausblenden.
t.en: Standard drinks can’t be edited, but you can hide them from the list if you don’t need them.
t.es: No puedes editar las bebidas estándar, pero puedes ocultarlas de la lista si no las necesitas.
t.fr: Les boissons standard ne peuvent pas être modifiées, mais tu peux les cacher de la liste si tu n'en as pas besoin.
t.hi: स्टैंडर्ड पेय एडिट नहीं किए जा सकते, पर ज़रूरत न हो तो उन्हें सूची से छिपा सकते हो।
t.id: Minuman standar tidak bisa diedit. Tapi kamu bisa menyembunyikannya dari daftar kalau tidak butuh.
t.it: Le bevande standard non possono essere modificate. Ma puoi nasconderle dall'elenco se non ne hai bisogno.
t.ja: 標準的なドリンクは編集できません。ただし、不要ならリストから非表示にできます。
t.ko: 기본 음료는 편집할 수 없습니다. 목록에서 필요하지 않은 음료를 숨길 수는 있습니다.
t.ms: Minuman standard tidak boleh diedit. Tetapi anda boleh menyembunyikannya daripada senarai jika anda tidak memerlukannya.
t.nb: Standarddrikker kan ikke redigeres, men du kan skjule dem fra listen hvis du ikke trenger dem.
t.nl: Standaard drankjes kunnen niet worden bewerkt, maar je kunt ze verbergen als je ze niet nodig hebt.
t.pl: Standardowe napoje nie mogą być edytowane. Możesz jednak ukryć je z listy, jeśli nie są Ci potrzebne.
t.pt_BR: Bebidas padrão não podem ser editadas. Mas você pode escondê-las da lista se não precisar delas.
t.ru: Стандартный напиток нельзя редактировать. Но ты можешь скрыть его из списка, если он не нужен.
t.sv: Standarddrycker kan inte redigeras. Men du kan dölja den från listan om du inte behöver den.
t.tr: Standart içecekler düzenlenemez, ihtiyacın yoksa listeden gizleyebilirsin.
t.vi: Không thể chỉnh sửa đồ uống tiêu chuẩn. Nhưng bạn có thể ẩn nó khỏi danh sách nếu không cần đến.
t.zh_CN: 标准饮料无法编辑。但如果您不需要该饮料,您可以将其从列表中隐藏。

context:
Surface: Create / edit beverage screen — caffeine and sugar amount-per-volume hint, shown in parentheses next to the section title.
  Type: paragraph (unit description).
  Context: Generic "<unit> per <volume>" template — describes how much caffeine or sugar a given volume of the beverage contains (currently "mg per 100 ml" / "g per 100 ml"). The reference volume is hardcoded to 100 in the call site; only the unit string is localized.
  Placeholders:
    %@ — substance unit, localized via `R.string.localizable.mg()` / `.g()`
    %@ — reference volume composed at the call site as `"100 " + R.string.localizable.ml()`; does not switch to fl oz for imperial users

en: You can't hide the last drink
context:
Surface: Settings → Create / edit drink screen — alert shown when the user tries to hide the last remaining visible beverage in their drinks catalog.
  Type: error message.
  Context: Prevents the user from ending up with zero visible beverages by hiding the only remaining one. "Hide" here is the in-app action (the beverage is not deleted permanently — it can be unhidden later). Despite the key name containing "Deletion", the action is hiding, not deleting; translator should follow the value ("hide"), not the key name.

t.da: Du kan ikke skjule den sidste drik
t.de: Du kannst das letzte Getränk nicht ausblenden
t.en: You can't hide the last drink
t.es: No puedes ocultar la última bebida
t.fr: Vous ne pouvez pas masquer la dernière boisson
t.hi: आख़िरी पेय छिपाया नहीं जा सकता
t.ja: 最後のドリンクは非表示にできません
t.ms: Anda tidak boleh menyembunyikan minuman terakhir
t.nb: Du kan ikke skjule den siste drikken
t.nl: Je kunt het laatste drankje niet verbergen
t.pt_BR: Você não pode esconder a última bebida
t.sv: Du kan inte dölja den sista drycken
t.vi: Bạn không thể ẩn đồ uống cuối
t.zh_CN: 您无法隐藏最后一款饮料

en: Each drink has a different hydration percentage and affects your body’s water balance differently. You can find out a drink’s ratio by consulting a nutritionist or searching online.
context:
Surface: Create / edit drink screen — explainer popup body shown when the user taps the info button next to the "Water balance" / hydration-coefficient field (CreateDrinkViewController showWaterBalanceAlert). Paired title "waterBalance".
  Type: paragraph (instructional).
  Context: Two short paragraphs separated by a blank line. Explains that different beverages contribute differently to body water balance (hydration percentage / coefficient varies by drink) and advises the user where to look up a drink's coefficient (nutritionist or Internet).

t.ar: لكل مشروب نسبة ترطيب مختلفة ويؤثر على التوازن المائي للجسم بطرق مختلفة. يُمكنك معرفة نسبة ترطيب المشروب عن طريق استشارة خبير تغذية أو البحث عبر الإنترنت.
t.da: Hver drik har sin egen hydreringsprocent og påvirker kroppens vandbalance forskelligt. Du kan finde en driks forhold ved at rådføre dig med en ernæringsekspert eller søge information online.
t.de: Jedes Getränk hat einen anderen Flüssigkeitsanteil und beeinflusst den Wasserhaushalt des Körpers auf unterschiedliche Weise. Den Koeffizienten eines Getränks kannst du herausfinden, indem du einen Ernährungsberater fragst oder online nachschaust.
t.en: Each drink has a different hydration percentage and affects your body’s water balance differently. You can find out a drink’s ratio by consulting a nutritionist or searching online.
t.es: Cada bebida tiene un porcentaje de hidratación diferente y afecta de forma distinta al equilibrio hídrico del organismo. Puedes consultar a un nutricionista o investigar en Internet para averiguar la proporción de la bebida.
t.fr: Chaque boisson possède un pourcentage d’hydratation spécifique et affecte différemment l’équilibre hydrique du corps. Vous pouvez connaître le taux propre à cette boisson en consultant un nutritionniste ou en faisant des recherches en ligne.
t.hi: हर पेय का हाइड्रेशन प्रतिशत अलग होता है और यह शरीर के पानी के संतुलन को अलग-अलग तरह से प्रभावित करता है। तुम पोषण विशेषज्ञ से सलाह लेकर या इंटरनेट पर देखकर पेय का अनुपात पता कर सकते हो।
t.id: Setiap minuman memiliki persentase hidrasi berbeda-beda dan memengaruhi keseimbangan air tubuh dengan cara yang berbeda. Anda dapat mengetahui rasio minuman dengan berkonsultasi kepada ahli gizi atau mencari informasi di Internet.
t.it: Ogni bevanda ha una diversa percentuale di idratazione e influisce in modo diverso sull'equilibrio idrico del corpo. Può scoprire l'indice della bevanda consultando un nutrizionista o cercando informazioni su Internet.
t.ja: ドリンクはそれぞれ水分の割合が異なり、身体の水分バランスに与える影響も異なります。 栄養士に相談したり、インターネットで情報を検索したりすれば、ドリンクの比率を知ることができます。
t.ko: 음료마다 수분 흡수율이 다르고 체내 수분 균형에 미치는 영향도 달라요. 영양사와 상담하거나 인터넷에서 음료의 수분 흡수율을 찾아볼 수 있어요.
t.ms: Setiap minuman mempunyai peratusan penghidratan yang berbeza dan memberi kesan kepada keseimbangan air badan dengan cara yang berbeza. Anda boleh mengetahui nisbah minuman dengan berunding dengan pakar pemakanan atau mencari maklumat di Internet.
t.nb: Hver drikk har sin egen hydreringsprosent og påvirker kroppens vannbalanse på ulike måter. Du kan finne ut drikkens prosent ved å spørre en ernæringsfysiolog eller søke på nettet.
t.nl: Elk drankje heeft een ander hydratatiepercentage en beïnvloedt de waterbalans van het lichaam op verschillende manieren. Je kunt de verhouding van het drankje achterhalen door een voedingsdeskundige te raadplegen of informatie op internet te zoeken.
t.pl: Każdy napój ma inny procent nawodnienia i w różny sposób wpływa na gospodarkę wodną organizmu. Możesz dowiedzieć się, jaki jest procent dla danego napoju, konsultując się z dietetykiem lub wyszukując informacji w Internecie.
t.pt_BR: Cada bebida tem uma porcentagem de hidratação diferente e afeta o balanço hídrico do corpo de formas distintas. Você pode descobrir a proporção da bebida consultando um nutricionista ou procurando informações na internet.
t.ru: Каждый напиток имеет свой процент гидратации и по-разному влияет на водный баланс организма. Узнать коэффициент напитка можно у диетолога или в интернете.
t.sv: Alla drycker har olika vattenprocent och påverkar kroppens vattenbalans på olika sätt. Du kan ta reda på dryckens andel genom att fråga en näringsexpert eller hitta information på internet.
t.tr: Her içeceğin farklı bir hidrasyon yüzdesi vardır ve vücudun su dengesini farklı şekillerde etkiler. Bir beslenme uzmanına danışarak ya da internetten bilgi edinerek içeceğin oranını öğrenebilirsiniz.
t.vi: Mỗi đồ uống có tỷ lệ giải khát khác nhau và ảnh hưởng đến cân bằng nước của cơ thể theo những cách khác nhau. Bạn có thể tìm hiểu tỷ lệ của đồ uống bằng cách hỏi ý kiến chuyên gia dinh dưỡng hoặc tìm thông tin trên Internet.
t.zh_CN: 每种饮料都有不同的含水量,会以不同的方式影响身体的水分平衡。 您可以通过咨询营养师或在网上查找资料来了解某种饮料的水合比例。

en: Track the amount of caffeine in your drink. Caffeine in mg per [%s].
context:
Surface: Create / edit drink screen — explainer popup body shown when the user taps the info button next to the caffeine field (CreateDrinkViewController showCaffeineAlert).
  Type: paragraph (instructional).
  Context: Two short sentences. First explains the purpose of the caffeine field (it lets the user track caffeine intake); second clarifies that the entered value is in milligrams per the given reference volume (e.g. "per 100 ml").
  Placeholders:
    %@ — reference volume with unit, currently assembled at the call site as localized "100 ml"
  Constraints: Trailing period on the second sentence.

t.ar: تتبع كمية الكافيين في مشروبك. كمية الكافيين بالملغ لكل [%s].
t.da: Hold øje med koffeinmængden. Mængden af koffein i mg per [%s].
t.de: Behalte den Überblick über die Koffeinmenge. Die Menge an Koffein in mg pro [%s].
t.en: Track the amount of caffeine in your drink. Caffeine in mg per [%s].
t.es: Lleva un registro de la cantidad de cafeína. La cantidad de cafeína en mg por [%s].
t.fr: Suis la quantité de caféine. La quantité de caféine est exprimée en mg par [%s].
t.hi: कैफीन की मात्रा का ध्यान रखो। प्रति [%s] मिलीग्राम में कैफीन।
t.id: Lacak jumlah kafein. Jumlah kafein dalam mg per [%s].
t.it: Tieni traccia della quantità di caffeina. La quantità di caffeina in mg per [%s].
t.ja: カフェイン量を把握しましょう。 カフェイン量はmg/[%s]です。
t.ko: 음료에 들어 있는 카페인 양을 기록하세요. 카페인은 [%s]당 mg 단위로 입력합니다.
t.ms: Jejak jumlah kafein. Jumlah kafein dalam mg setiap [%s].
t.nb: Hold styr på koffeinmengden. Mengden av koffein i mg per [%s].
t.nl: Houd de hoeveelheid cafeïne bij. De hoeveelheid cafeïne in mg per [%s].
t.pl: Zwracaj uwagę na ilość kofeiny. Ilość kofeiny w mg na [%s].
t.pt_BR: Acompanhe a quantidade de cafeína. A quantidade de cafeína em mg por [%s].
t.ru: Отслеживай количество кофеина. Количество кофеина в мг на [%s].
t.sv: Håll koll på mängden koffein. Mängden koffein i mg per [%s].
t.tr: Kafein miktarını takip edin. [%s] başına mg cinsinden kafein miktarı.
t.vi: Theo dõi lượng caffeine. Lượng caffeine tính bằng mg mỗi [%s].
t.zh_CN: 记录咖啡因的含量。 每 [%s] 所含咖啡因的毫克数。

en: Create your own drinks and customize their properties
context:
Surface: Paywall / subscription benefits list — subtitle under the "Own drinks" benefit item.
  Type: paragraph (benefit description).
  Context: Describes the premium benefit of creating personalized custom drinks with adjustable properties (color, name, hydration coefficient, caffeine, sugar). Paired with the "Own drinks" title.

t.da: Lav dine egne drikke og tilpas deres egenskaber
t.de: Erstelle deine eigenen Getränke und passe deren Eigenschaften an
t.en: Create your own drinks and customize their properties
t.es: Crea tus propias bebidas y personaliza sus propiedades
t.fr: Crée tes propres boissons et personnalise leurs propriétés
t.hi: अपने खुद के पेय बनाओ और उनके गुण कस्टमाइज़ करो
t.id: Buat minumanmu sendiri dan sesuaikan propertinya
t.it: Crea le tue bevande e personalizza le loro proprietà
t.ja: 自分だけのドリンクを作って成分をカスタマイズしましょう
t.ko: 나만의 음료를 만들고 속성을 자유롭게 설정해 보세요
t.ms: Cipta minuman anda sendiri dan sesuaikan kandungannya
t.nb: Lag dine egne drikker og tilpass egenskapene deres.
t.nl: Maak je eigen drankjes en pas hun eigenschappen aan
t.pl: Twórz własne napoje i dobieraj ich właściwości
t.pt_BR: Crie suas próprias bebidas e personalize as propriedades delas
t.ru: Создавай собственные напитки и настраивай их параметры
t.sv: Skapa egna drycker och anpassa deras egenskaper
t.tr: Kendi içeceklerini yarat ve özelliklerini özelleştir
t.vi: Tạo đồ uống của riêng bạn và tùy chỉnh thuộc tính của chúng
t.zh_CN: 创建你自己的饮料并自定义其属性

context:
Surface: Paywall / subscription benefits list — title of the "create your own drinks" premium benefit item; subscription compare table row; onboarding subscription card.
  Type: benefit title.
  Context: Title of the premium benefit that lets the user create custom personalized drinks. Paired with the subtitle "Create your own drinks and customize their properties".
  Constraints: Keep short to fit benefit row title; no trailing period.

en: You can now create your own drinks with My Water Premium! Just go to Settings and open the "[%s]" section.
context:
Surface: Currently no live Swift call-sites (orphan key — legacy onboarding / upsell explainer for the own-drinks premium feature).
  Type: paragraph (feature explainer).
  Context: Tells the user that after activating My Water Premium they can create custom drinks, and directs them to the settings section whose localized name is interpolated as the placeholder.
  Placeholders:
    %@ — localized name of the settings section that opens the own-drinks editor (e.g. "Own drinks")
  Tone: encouraging.

t.ar: يُمكنك الآن صنع مشروباتك المخصصة باستخدام My Water Premium! لتتمكن من ذلك، اذهب إلى إعدادات التطبيق واختر قسم "[%s]".
t.da: Du kan nu lave dine egne drikke med My Water Premium! Gå til Indstillinger, og åbn afsnittet "[%s]".
t.de: Mit My Water Premium kannst du jetzt deine eigenen Getränke erstellen! Öffne dazu die App-Einstellungen und wähle den Bereich „[%s]“ aus.
t.en: You can now create your own drinks with My Water Premium! Just go to Settings and open the "[%s]" section.
t.es: Ya puedes crear tus propias bebidas con My Water Premium. Para hacerlo, ve a los ajustes de la app y selecciona la sección "[%s]".
t.fr: Avec My Water Premium, vous pouvez maintenant créer vos propres boissons ! Pour ce faire, allez dans les paramètres de l’application, puis dans la section "[%s]".
t.hi: My Water Premium के साथ अब तुम अपने खुद के पेय बना सकते हो! इसके लिए ऐप की सेटिंग में जाओ और "[%s]" सेक्शन चुनो.
t.id: Sekarang kamu bisa membuat minuman sendiri dengan My Water Premium! Untuk melakukannya, buka pengaturan aplikasi dan pilih bagian "[%s]".
t.it: Ora puoi creare le tue bevande con My Water Premium! Per fare ciò, vai alle impostazioni dell'applicazione e seleziona la sezione "[%s]".
t.ja: 自分だけのドリンクをMy Water Premiumで作れるようになりました！ ドリンクを作るには、アプリ設定に進んで「[%s]」の項目を選択してください。
t.ko: 이제 My Water Premium으로 나만의 음료를 만들 수 있어요! 만들려면 앱 설정으로 가서 "[%s]" 섹션을 선택하세요.
t.ms: Anda kini boleh mencipta minuman anda sendiri dengan My Water Premium! Untuk melakukan ini, pergi ke tetapan aplikasi dan pilih bahagian "[%s]".
t.nb: Du kan nå lage dine egne drikker med Mitt Vann Premium! Gå til appinnstillingene og velg "[%s]".
t.nl: Je kunt nu je eigen drankjes maken met My Water Premium! Ga hiervoor naar de app-instellingen en selecteer de sectie "[%s]".
t.pl: Dzięki aplikacji My Water Premium możesz teraz tworzyć własne napoje! Aby to zrobić, przejdź do ustawień aplikacji i wybierz sekcję "[%s]".
t.pt_BR: Agora você pode criar suas próprias bebidas com o My Water Premium! Para isso, acesse as configurações do aplicativo e selecione a seção "[%s]".
t.ru: Теперь с My Water Premium можно создавать свои собственные напитки! Для этого зайди в настройки приложения и выбери раздел "[%s]".
t.sv: Nu kan du skapa dina egna drycker med Mitt Vatten Premium! För att göra det, gå till appens inställningar och välj avsnittet "[%s]".
t.tr: My Water Premium ile artık kendi içeceklerini yaratabilirsin! Bunu yapmak için uygulama ayarlarına gir ve "[%s]" bölümünü seç.
t.vi: Giờ đây, bạn có thể tạo đồ uống của riêng mình với My Water Premium! Để làm điều này, hãy đi đến cài đặt ứng dụng và chọn phần "[%s]".
t.zh_CN: 你现在可以使用 My Water Premium 创建自己的饮品！ 要创建，请前往应用设置，然后选择"[%s]"版块。

context:
Surface: Main screen — discount offer cell title; paywall / subscription system-style header cell title; discount-offer popup title.
  Type: paragraph (marketing claim).
  Context: Personal discount offer headline — promotes a percentage discount on the premium subscription (e.g. "Special discount -50% for you!"). Often paired with the subtitle "Start drink more water...".
  Placeholders:
    %@ — discount percentage with sign (e.g. "-50%", "30%")
  Tone: encouraging / promotional.

t.fr: Pour toi, une remise spéciale de [%s] !
t.ja: あなたのために特別な[%s]割引！
t.vi: Ưu đãi đặc biệt [%s] cho bạn!
t.zh_CN: 专为你准备的 [%s] 特别折扣！

en: Start drinking more water. Your body will thank you. We will help!
context:
Surface: Main screen — special discount cell, supporting subtitle text under the discount title (specialDiscountCellTitle).
  Type: paragraph (marketing claim).
  Context: Friendly inviting subtitle that supports the discount offer — frames the discount as the app helping the user drink more water. Note: the English source has a minor grammar slip ("Start drink" instead of "Start drinking"); render target-language grammar naturally and do not propagate the slip.
  Tone: encouraging / friendly.

t.ar: ابدأ بشرب المزيد من الماء. سيشكرك جسمك. سنساعدك!
t.da: Begynd at drikke mere vand. Din krop vil takke dig. Vi vil hjælpe dig!
t.de: Fang an, mehr Wasser zu trinken. Dein Körper wird es dir danken. Wir helfen dir dabei!
t.en: Start drinking more water. Your body will thank you. We will help!
t.es: Comienza a beber más agua. Tu cuerpo te lo agradecerá. ¡Te ayudaremos!
t.fr: Commence à boire plus d'eau. Ton corps te remerciera. On t'aidera !
t.hi: ज़्यादा पानी पीना शुरू करो. तुम्हारा शरीर शुक्रिया कहेगा. हम मदद करेंगे!
t.id: Mulai minum lebih banyak air. Tubuhmu akan berterima kasih. Kami akan membantu!
t.it: Inizia a bere più acqua. Il tuo corpo ti ringrazierà. Ti aiuteremo!
t.ja: 水をたくさん飲むようにしましょう。体が喜びます。私たちがお手伝いします！
t.ko: 물을 더 자주 마시면, 몸이 고마워할 거예요. 저희가 도울게요!
t.ms: Mula minum lebih banyak air. Badan awak akan berterima kasih. Kami akan membantu!
t.nb: Begynn å drikke mer vann. Kroppen din vil takke deg. Vi hjelper!
t.nl: Begin met meer water drinken. Je lichaam zal je dankbaar zijn. Wij helpen je!
t.pl: Zacznij pić więcej wody. Twoje ciało Ci podziękuje. Pomożemy!
t.pt_BR: Comece a beber mais água. Seu corpo vai agradecer. Nós vamos ajudar!
t.ru: Начни пить больше воды. Твоё тело отблагодарит тебя. Мы поможем!
t.sv: Börja dricka mer vatten. Din kropp kommer att tacka dig. Vi hjälper dig!
t.tr: Daha fazla su içmeye başla. Vücudun sana teşekkür edecek. Sana yardımcı olacağız!
t.vi: Hãy bắt đầu uống nhiều nước hơn. Cơ thể sẽ cảm ơn bạn. Chúng tôi sẽ giúp bạn!
t.zh_CN: 开始多喝水。你的身体会感谢你。我们来帮你！

context:
Surface: Progress / activity indicator label (currently no live Swift call-sites — preserved for legacy / pending sync UI).
  Type: paragraph (status message).
  Context: Tells the user that the app is currently synchronizing data with the server / cloud. Present-continuous status framing ("in progress").

t.vi: Đang đồng bộ hóa dữ liệu

en: You need an active My Water Premium subscription to create or edit your own drinks
context:

  Constraints: 1 short sentence; the slash-pair "edit/create" can be re-ordered in target language to read naturally.
  Tone: informative, not aggressive.

context:
Surface: Paywall — badge / label on a subscription option showing the discount percentage.
  Type: badge / label.
  Context: Short badge showing how much the user saves on the subscription (e.g. "50% off!", "30% off!"). Shown on yearly / discounted tier buttons.
  Placeholders:
    %@ — discount percent, already formatted with percent sign (e.g. "50%", "30%")
  Constraints: Keep very short — fits a compact badge.

Surface: Apphud rule-based push notification, title.
  Type: notification title.
  Context: Title of an Apphud server-driven push sent when the user has signaled dissatisfaction with the app (e.g. low rating prompt). Opens a follow-up feedback flow. Phrased as a soft rhetorical question.

t.ja: アプリがお気に召さない？
t.nl: Vind je de app niet leuk?

en: Help us improve. Tell us what’s not working for you.
context:
Surface: Push notification / Apphud rule-based feedback prompt (currently unreferenced in Swift code, preserved for legacy / Apphud-server-driven push text).
  Type: notification body.
  Context: Follow-up message after the user signaled the app is not helping (see "didntLikeAppApphudPush"); asks for actionable feedback on what's wrong and how to improve.
  Tone: neutral / informative — direct request, not accusatory.

t.ar: ساعدنا على التحسّن. أخبرنا بما لا يعمل عندك.
t.da: Hjælp os med at blive bedre. Fortæl os, hvad der ikke fungerer for dig.
t.de: Hilf uns, besser zu werden. Sag uns, was bei dir nicht funktioniert.
t.en: Help us improve. Tell us what’s not working for you.
t.es: Cuéntanos qué no te funciona. ¿Cómo podemos mejorar?
t.fr: Aide-nous à nous améliorer. Dis-nous ce qui ne fonctionne pas pour toi.
t.hi: हमें बेहतर बनने में मदद करो। बताओ क्या काम नहीं कर रहा।
t.id: Bantu kami jadi lebih baik. Beri tahu kami apa yang tidak berfungsi untukmu.

t.nb: Hjelp oss å bli bedre. Fortell oss hva som ikke fungerer for deg.
t.nl: Help ons beter te worden. Vertel ons wat er voor jou niet werkt.
t.pl: Pomóż nam się rozwijać. Powiedz, co Ci nie działa.
t.pt_BR: Ajude-nos a melhorar. Conte o que não está funcionando para você.
t.ru: Помоги нам стать лучше - расскажи, что не работает.
t.sv: Hjälp oss att bli bättre. Berätta vad som inte fungerar för dig.
t.tr: Daha iyi olmamıza yardım et. Neyin işe yaramadığını bize anlat.
t.vi: Hãy cho chúng tôi biết điều gì chưa ổn để chúng tôi cải thiện nhé.
t.zh_CN: 帮助我们改进。请告诉我们哪里对你不好用。

context:

t.da: Indtast e-mailadressen til din konto i appen. Vi sender dig et link til at nulstille din adgangskode.

t.pt_BR: Digite o endereço de e-mail de sua conta no aplicativo. Nós lhe enviaremos um link para redefinir sua senha.

t.vi: Nhập địa chỉ email của tài khoản của bạn vào ứng dụng. Chúng tôi sẽ gửi cho bạn một liên kết để đặt lại mật khẩu của bạn.

Surface: Friends list screen — incoming friend request cell, secondary action button (paired with the "Add" accept button).
  Type: button label.
  Context: Imperative verb. Rejects / declines an incoming friend request.
  Constraints: Keep short (≤10 chars). No trailing period.

Surface: Beverages statistic screen — empty state title when the user has not added any drinks in the selected statistic period.

  Context: Shown when the beverages breakdown chart has no data for the selected period.

Surface: Friends list screen — empty state title (no friends added yet).

context:

en: Please check your connection and try again

  Context: Action-oriented body asking the user to verify their connection and retry. Paired with the title key "connectionErrorTitle" — translate as a pair so the two lines read coherently. Tone is polite and action-oriented.

context:
Surface: Onboarding weather / climate selection screen — stage title (`OnboardingWeatherViewController`). Also used as the title of the "Weather" parameter card in the goal-calculation parameters screen (`OnboardingChooseCalculationViewModel`).
  Type: screen title / parameter card title.
  Context: Single-word noun naming the climate / weather parameter that contributes to the daily water goal calculation. The screen lets the user pick between climate options (cold / temperate / hot).
  Constraints: Keep short (single word where possible).

Surface: Onboarding weather screen — title of the "normal / temperate climate" option card (paired with subtitle key "normalClimateDescription"; sibling option "hotClimate" / "hotClimateDescription").
  Type: option label.
  Context: Title of the default climate option presented during onboarding — selecting it leaves the daily-goal calculation at its baseline (no hot-climate adjustment). Used only in this climate-selector context, not as a generic settings value (the app-wide "On" / settings "Normal" status is a different key).
  Constraints: Keep short — single word fitting an option card title.

Surface: Onboarding weather screen — subtitle under the "Normal" climate option card (paired with title key "normal").
  Type: option description.
  Context: Short description clarifying that the "Normal" option means a temperate climate and is the default choice if the user does not live in a hot region. Sibling of "hotClimateDescription".
  Constraints: Keep concise — fits one line under the option title. "(default)" annotation is informational; preserve the parenthetical convention.

t.ja: 温暖な気候 (デフォルト)

context:
Surface: Onboarding weather screen — title of the "hot climate" option card (OnboardingWeatherViewController) paired with subtitle key "hotClimateDescription"; sibling option "normal" / "normalClimateDescription". Affects daily water goal calculation (DailyNormService).
  Type: option / card title.
  Context: Short adjective describing a hot climate region — selected by users living in sunny / hot areas to bump up the recommended daily water amount. Capitalized as a card title.
  Constraints: Keep very short (single adjective, ≤8 chars).

en: Lots of sunny and hot days
context:
Surface: Onboarding weather screen — subtitle under the "hotClimate" option card (OnboardingWeatherViewController). Sibling of "normalClimateDescription".
  Type: option / card description.
  Context: One-line description clarifying that the "Hot" option means many sunny and hot days. Sits under the "Hot" title in the climate selection card.
  Constraints: Keep concise — fits one line under the option title.

t.da: Masser af solrige og varme dage

context:
Surface: Onboarding drinks survey — screen stage title.
  Type: screen title.
  Context: Title of the onboarding step where the user selects which sugary drinks (soda, sweet juices, etc.) they consume. Drives initial drink-habits profile.
  Constraints: Keep short to fit the stage title area; no trailing period.

Surface: Onboarding "Sugary beverages" and "Coffee and energy drinks" questionnaire screens — frequency option title (selectable card / row).
  Type: option title.
  Context: Lowest-frequency option in the "how often do you consume X" picker (paired with `almostNeverSubtitle`). Used both for sugary drinks (`OnboardingDrinksViewModel`) and for coffee / energy drinks (`OnboardingCoffeeViewModel`) — affects daily water goal calculation.
  Constraints: Keep short (≤15 chars).

Surface: Onboarding "Sugary beverages" and "Coffee and energy drinks" questionnaire screens — description shown under the "Almost never" option title.
  Type: option subtitle.
  Context: Clarifies what frequency "Almost never" actually means in this questionnaire (essentially zero up to a few times per month). The "/" separates two equivalent example frequencies.
  Constraints: Single short line; fits as subtitle under an option title.

t.hi: कभी नहीं / महीने में कई बार

t.vi: Không bao giờ / vài lần một tháng

context:
Surface: Onboarding "Sugary beverages", "Coffee and energy drinks", and fruits / vegetables questionnaire screens — description shown under the "Rarely" option title.
  Type: option subtitle.
  Context: Clarifies what frequency "Rarely" means in these questionnaires (low-frequency weekly consumption). Used for sugary drinks (`OnboardingDrinksViewModel`), coffee / energy drinks (`OnboardingCoffeeViewModel`), and fruits / vegetables (`OnboardingFruitsViewController`) — affects daily water goal calculation.
  Constraints: Single short line; fits as subtitle under an option title.

Surface: Onboarding drinks / coffee / fruits questionnaire option subtitle for "daily" frequency, and Settings → Notifications repeat-days summary when reminders fire every day of the week.
  Type: option subtitle / settings value.
  Context: Time-frequency label meaning "occurs each day". In onboarding it sits under an option title as the example frequency for drinks / coffee / fruits, not the Activity workout screen. In notifications settings it shows as the current value when all 7 weekdays are selected.
  Constraints: Keep short — fits as a subtitle / value on one line.

Surface: Onboarding questionnaire option subtitle for "often" frequency — sugary drinks (`OnboardingDrinksViewModel`), coffee / energy drinks (`OnboardingCoffeeViewModel`), and fruits / vegetables (`OnboardingFruitsViewController`) screens.
  Type: option subtitle.
  Context: Clarifies what frequency "Often" means in these questionnaires — multiple servings within a single day. Sibling of "almostNeverSubtitle" / "rarelySubtitle" / "everyDay".
  Constraints: Single short line; fits as subtitle under an option title.

Surface: Onboarding "Coffee and energy drinks" questionnaire screen (OnboardingCoffeeViewController) — stage title above the frequency options.
  Type: screen title.
  Context: Title of the onboarding step where the user picks how often they drink coffee and energy drinks (affects daily water goal — caffeine has a mild diuretic effect). Plural "drinks" applies to both coffee and energy beverages.

context:
Surface: Onboarding "Vegetables and fruits" questionnaire screen (`OnboardingFruitsViewController`) — stage title above the frequency options.
  Type: screen title.
  Context: Title of the onboarding step where the user picks how often they eat vegetables and fruits (affects daily water goal — produce contains water and contributes to hydration). Key has historical spaces in identifier; R.swift exposes it as `vegetablesAndFruits()`. Do not rename the key.
  Constraints: Keep short to fit the stage title area; no trailing period.

Surface: Settings → user profile row — colored CTA shown to logged-out users (replaces the user name).
  Type: button label.
  Context: Tappable CTA opening the authorization flow (sign-in or sign-up). Covers both actions in one label since the destination screen offers both options.
  Constraints: Keep reasonably short — fits a single row in Settings; long translations may wrap.

t.hi: साइन इन या साइन अप करो
t.ja: サインインまたは登録

en: Based on your parameters, your estimated goal is [%.1f] [%s] of water. Your doctor can determine the exact goal for you.
context:
Surface: Onboarding goal-calculation result screen (`OnboardingNormViewModel`, `OnboardingNormCircleViewController`) — shown for fractional units like litres.
  Type: paragraph (educational).
  Context: Explains the personalized daily water goal computed from the user's parameters; suggests consulting a doctor for the exact value. Fractional-digit variant (used for litres). Compare `youShouldDrink0Digits` for whole-number variant (ml / fl oz).
  Placeholders:
    %.1f — daily goal amount with 1 decimal (e.g. 1.2)
    %@ — measurement unit ("l" / "ml" / "fl oz")

t.ar: بناءً على معاييرك، الكمية المستهدفة من الماء هي [%.1f] [%s] تقريبًا. ويمكن لطبيبك تحديد الكمية الدقيقة المناسبة لك.
t.da: Baseret på dine parametre er dit estimerede mål [%.1f] [%s] vand. Din læge kan fastslå dit nøjagtige mål.
t.de: Das geschätzte Ziel basierend auf Ihren Parametern beträgt [%.1f] [%s] Wasser. Ihr Arzt kann das genaue Ziel für Sie bestimmen.
t.en: Based on your parameters, your estimated goal is [%.1f] [%s] of water. Your doctor can determine the exact goal for you.
t.es: La meta estimada en función de sus parámetros es [%.1f] [%s] de agua. Su médico puede determinar la meta exacta para usted.
t.fr: L'objectif estimé en fonction de vos paramètres est [%.1f] [%s] d'eau. Votre médecin peut déterminer l'objectif exact pour vous.
t.hi: आपके पैरामीटर के आधार पर पानी का अनुमानित लक्ष्य [%.1f] [%s] है. आपके डॉक्टर आपके लिए सटीक लक्ष्य तय कर सकते हैं.
t.id: Perkiraan target berdasarkan parameter Anda adalah [%.1f] [%s] air. Dokter Anda dapat menentukan target pastinya.
t.it: L'obiettivo stimato in base ai Suoi parametri è [%.1f] [%s] di acqua. Il Suo medico può determinare l'obiettivo esatto per Lei.
t.ja: あなたの情報から算出した1日の目標水分量の目安は [%.1f] [%s] です。正確な量については医師にご相談ください。
t.ko: 입력하신 정보를 바탕으로 한 예상 목표는 물 [%.1f] [%s]예요. 정확한 목표는 의사와 상담해 보세요.
t.ms: Matlamat anggaran berdasarkan parameter anda ialah [%.1f] [%s] air. Doktor anda boleh menentukan matlamat yang tepat untuk anda.
t.nb: Beregnet mål basert på parameterne dine er [%.1f] [%s] vann. Legen din kan finne det nøyaktige målet for deg.
t.nl: Op basis van uw parameters is uw geschatte doel [%.1f] [%s] water. Uw arts kan het exacte doel voor u bepalen.
t.pl: Szacowany cel na podstawie Twoich parametrów to [%.1f] [%s] wody. Twój lekarz może określić cel dostosowany do Twoich zapotrzebowań.
t.pt_BR: A meta estimada com base nos seus parâmetros é de [%.1f] [%s] de água. O seu médico pode definir a meta exata para você.
t.ru: Ориентировочная цель на основе ваших параметров: [%.1f] [%s] воды. Ваш врач может определить точную цель для вас.
t.sv: Uppskattat mål baserat på dina parametrar är [%.1f] [%s] vatten. Din läkare kan fastställa det exakta målet åt dig.
t.tr: Parametrelerine göre tahmini hedef [%.1f] [%s] su. Doktorun senin için kesin hedefi belirleyebilir.
t.vi: Mục tiêu ước tính dựa trên các hệ số của bạn là [%.1f] [%s] nước. Bác sĩ của bạn có thể xác định mục tiêu chính xác dành cho bạn.
t.zh_CN: 根据您的参数估计的饮水目标是 [%.1f] [%s]。您的医生可以为您确定确切的目标。

context:
Surface: Settings screen — "Join community" cell label above the row of MyWater brand social-network icons (Facebook, Instagram, Twitter).
  Type: section label.
  Context: Invitation header introducing the app's official social-network accounts that the user can open from this cell. "Our" = MyWater's (the app's). Title-cased label.
  Constraints: Keep short (one line, fits a cell label).

t.vi: Mạng xã hội của chúng tôi

context:
Surface: Onboarding height screen / scale slider — unit toggle button label (shown when metric system is selected).
  Type: unit abbreviation.
  Context: Short abbreviation for "centimeters" — used as the units indicator next to the height slider when the user is in the metric system.
  Constraints: Keep abbreviation form (2-3 chars); use locale-standard abbreviation ("см" in ru, "cm" in en/de/fr, etc.).

Surface: Onboarding height screen / scale slider — unit toggle button label (shown when imperial system is selected).
  Type: unit abbreviation.
  Context: Imperial height units indicator — shown next to the height slider when the user is in feet/inches. Compare `centimeters` for metric variant.
  Constraints: Short label fitting next to the slider. Translate to the locale-standard imperial height units phrasing.

Surface: Onboarding age screen (`OnboardingAgeViewController`) — stage title above the age picker.
  Type: screen title.
  Context: Prompts the user to pick their age (parameter that contributes to the daily water goal calculation). Note: the key name reads "yourAge" but the English value is "Choose your age" — keep the imperative "choose your …" framing in translation (matches sibling "yourHeight" / `OnboardingHeightViewController`).

Surface: Onboarding height screen (`OnboardingHeightViewController`) — stage title above the height slider; also used as the label for the height row in `ScaleSliderView`.
  Type: screen title / row label.
  Context: Prompts the user to pick their height (parameter that contributes to the daily water goal calculation). Note: the key name reads "yourHeight" but the English value is "Choose your height" — keep the imperative "choose your …" framing in translation (matches sibling "yourAge" / `OnboardingAgeViewController`).

Surface: Onboarding name screen (`OnboardingNameViewController`) — stage title, text-field label, accessibility label, and placeholder for the name input.
  Type: screen title / field label / placeholder.
  Context: Single noun phrase used both as the screen title and as the input field placeholder asking the user to enter their first name. Unlike `yourAge` / `yourHeight` (imperative "choose your …"), this one is a noun phrase ("Your name") because it labels a free-text input rather than a picker.

Surface: Onboarding weather / climate selection screen — option title for cold weather.
  Type: option title.
  Context: Selectable climate option meaning "Cold climate"; affects daily water goal calculation. Paired with subtitle "Few sunny days, rainy" (`coldClimateDescription`).
  Constraints: Keep short (≤10 chars on narrow screens).

Surface: Onboarding weather / climate selection screen — subtitle under the "Cold" climate option (OnboardingWeatherViewController, paired with the "coldClimate" title).
  Type: option subtitle.
  Context: Short descriptor characterising a cold climate ("few sunny days, rainy"). Two short noun phrases joined by a comma; no verb.
  Constraints: Keep concise — fits as a one-line subtitle under the option title.

t.ja: 晴れの日が少なく、雨が多い

en: Send notifications after reaching your goal
context:
Surface: Onboarding notifications setup step (`NotificationsHorizontalIntervalViewController`) and Settings → Notifications interval popup (`NotificationsPopupIntervalsViewController`) — toggle row title.
  Type: setting label.
  Context: Title of the switch that controls whether the app continues to send reminder notifications after the user has already reached their daily water goal for the day. "The goal" refers to the daily water intake target.

t.da: Send notifikationer efter at have nået målet
t.de: Benachrichtigungen nach Erreichen des Ziels senden
t.en: Send notifications after reaching your goal
t.es: Enviar notificaciones tras lograr el objetivo
t.fr: Envoyer des notifications après avoir atteint l'objectif
t.hi: लक्ष्य तक पहुँचने के बाद नोटिफिकेशन भेजो
t.it: Invia notifiche dopo aver raggiunto l'obiettivo
t.ja: 目標達成後に通知を送信
t.nb: Send varsler etter at målet er oppnådd
t.nl: Meldingen verzenden na het bereiken van het doel
t.pt_BR: Enviar notificações após alcançar o objetivo
t.sv: Skicka aviseringar när du har nått ditt mål
t.vi: Gửi thông báo khi đã đạt mục tiêu

en: Send a notification if a drink was added recently
context:
Surface: Notifications settings — toggle row title (onboarding notifications step and main settings).
  Type: setting label.
  Context: Title of the switch that controls whether reminder notifications are still sent shortly after the user has just added a drink. Despite the literal English wording, the setting answers "should we still notify after a recent drink?".

t.ar: أرسل إشعاراً إذا تمت إضافة مشروب مؤخراً
t.da: Send en notifikation, hvis en drik blev tilføjet for nylig
t.de: Eine Benachrichtigung senden, wenn ein Getränk kürzlich hinzugefügt wurde
t.en: Send a notification if a drink was added recently
t.es: Enviar una notificación si se añadió una bebida recientemente
t.fr: Recevoir une notification si une boisson a été ajoutée récemment
t.hi: हाल ही में कोई पेय जोड़ा गया हो तो नोटिफिकेशन भेजो
t.id: Kirim pemberitahuan jika ada minuman yang baru saja ditambahkan
t.it: Invia una notifica se una bevanda è stata aggiunta di recente
t.ja: 最近飲み物が追加された場合に通知を送信
t.ms: Hantar notifikasi jika minuman ditambahkan baru-baru ini
t.nb: Send et varsel hvis en drikke nylig ble lagt til
t.nl: Stuur een melding als er onlangs een drankje is toegevoegd
t.pl: Wyślij powiadomienie, jeśli niedawno dodano napój
t.pt_BR: Enviar uma notificação se uma bebida foi adicionada recentemente
t.ru: Отправлять уведомление, если напиток был добавлен недавно
t.sv: Skicka en avisering om en dryck nyligen lagts till
t.tr: Yakın zamanda bir içecek eklendiyse bildirim gönder
t.vi: Gửi thông báo nếu một đồ uống vừa được thêm gần đây
t.zh_CN: 如果最近添加了饮料,请发送通知

t.es: Personaje de la pantalla de inicio
t.ja: ホーム画面のキャラクター

en: Choose a character for your Home screen. It will help you drink more water.
context:
Surface: Settings → Characters picker screen — description paragraph above the sectioned grid of mascots.
  Type: paragraph (subtitle).
  Context: Explains to the user that the selected character appears on the home screen and motivates them to drink more water.

t.ar: اختر شخصية للشاشة الرئيسية للتطبيق، مما سيساعدك على شرب المزيد من الماء.
t.da: Vælg en karakter til din startskærm, som vil hjælpe dig med at drikke mere vand.
t.de: Wähle einen Charakter für deinen Startbildschirm, der dir hilft, mehr Wasser zu trinken.
t.en: Choose a character for your Home screen. It will help you drink more water.
t.es: Elige un personaje para la pantalla de inicio de la aplicación, que te ayudará a beber más agua.
t.fr: Choisis un personnage pour l'écran d'accueil de l'application, qui t'aidera à boire plus d'eau.
t.hi: होम स्क्रीन के लिए एक कैरेक्टर चुनो, जो तुम्हें ज़्यादा पानी पीने में मदद करेगा।
t.id: Pilih karakter untuk layar Beranda aplikasi yang akan membantumu minum lebih banyak air.
t.it: Scegli un personaggio per la schermata Home dell'applicazione, che ti aiuterà a bere più acqua.
t.ja: ホーム画面に表示するキャラクターを選びましょう。もっと水を飲むのに役立ちます。
t.ko: 홈 화면에 표시할 캐릭터를 선택하세요. 물을 더 마시는 데 도움이 돼요.
t.ms: Pilih watak untuk skrin Utama aplikasi, yang akan membantu anda minum lebih banyak air.
t.nb: Velg en figur for startskjermen. Den hjelper deg å drikke mer vann.
t.nl: Kies een personage voor je startscherm. Het helpt je om meer water te drinken.
t.pl: Wybierz postać na ekranie głównym aplikacji, która pomoże Ci pić więcej wody.
t.pt_BR: Escolha um personagem para a tela inicial do aplicativo, que ajudará você a beber mais água.
t.ru: Выбери персонажа для главного экрана приложения, который поможет тебе пить больше воды.
t.sv: Välj en karaktär för appens startskärm, som hjälper dig att dricka mer vatten.
t.tr: Uygulamanın Ana ekranı için daha fazla su içmene yardımcı olacak bir karakter seç.
t.vi: Chọn một nhân vật cho Màn hình chính của ứng dụng, điều này sẽ giúp bạn uống nhiều nước hơn.
t.zh_CN: 为应用程序的主屏幕选择一个角色，这将帮助你喝更多的水。

en: Hi! Now you can choose a character in My Water to help you drink more water. Pick the style you like best!
context:
Surface: Characters feature-introduction popup shown once when the character picker becomes available.
  Type: paragraph (feature announcement).
  Context: Greets the user and announces that they can now choose a Home screen character (mascot) in My Water that will help them drink more water, and pick the character style they prefer.
  Tone: encouraging, friendly.

t.ar: أهلًا! يُمكنك الآن اختيار شخصية في My Water لتساعدك على شرب المزيد من الماء. يُمكنك اختيار مظهر الشخصية بنفسك!
t.da: Hej! Nu kan du vælge en karakter i Mit vand, som hjælper dig med at drikke mere vand. Du kan selv vælge karakterens stil!
t.de: Hallo! Jetzt kannst du in My Water einen Charakter auswählen, der dir hilft, mehr Wasser zu trinken. Du kannst den Stil des Charakters selbst wählen!
t.en: Hi! Now you can choose a character in My Water to help you drink more water. Pick the style you like best!
t.es: ¡Hola! Ahora puedes elegir un personaje en My Water que te ayudará a beber más agua. Puedes seleccionar el estilo de personaje que quieras.
t.fr: Salut ! Tu peux maintenant choisir un personnage dans My Water qui t'aidera à boire plus d'eau. Tu peux choisir le style de personnage que tu veux !
t.hi: नमस्ते! अब तुम My Water में एक कैरेक्टर चुन सकते हो जो तुम्हें ज़्यादा पानी पीने में मदद करेगा। तुम अपने लिए कैरेक्टर की स्टाइल चुन सकते हो!
t.id: Hai! Sekarang kamu bisa memilih karakter di My Water yang akan membantumu minum lebih banyak air. Kamu bisa memilih gaya karaktermu sendiri!
t.it: Ciao! Ora in My Water puoi scegliere un personaggio che ti aiuterà a bere più acqua. Puoi scegliere tu stesso lo stile del personaggio!
t.ja: こんにちは！My Water では、もっと水を飲むのを助けてくれるキャラクターを選べるようになりました。お好みのスタイルを選んでくださいね！
t.ko: 안녕하세요! 이제 «My Water»에서 물을 더 마시도록 도와주는 캐릭터를 고를 수 있어요. 원하는 스타일의 캐릭터를 선택해 보세요!
t.ms: Hai! Kini awak boleh memilih watak dalam My Water yang akan membantu awak minum lebih banyak air. Awak boleh memilih gaya watak sendiri!
t.nb: Hei! Nå kan du velge en figur i Mitt Vann som hjelper deg til å drikke mer vann. Du kan velge din egen figur!
t.nl: Hoi! Je kunt nu in My Water een personage kiezen dat je helpt om meer water te drinken. Je kunt zelf de stijl van het personage kiezen!
t.pl: Cześć! Teraz w My Water można wybrać postać, która pomoże Ci pić więcej wody. Wybierz styl postaci dla siebie!
t.pt_BR: Olá! Agora você pode escolher um personagem em My Water que ajudará você a beber mais água. Você mesmo pode selecionar o estilo do personagem!
t.ru: Привет! Теперь в приложении "Моя вода" появился персонаж, который будет помогать тебе пить больше воды. Ты можешь выбрать вид персонажа для себя!
t.sv: Hej! Nu kan du välja en karaktär i My Water som hjälper dig att dricka mer vatten. Du kan själv välja stil på karaktären!
t.tr: Merhaba! Artık My Water'da daha fazla su içmene yardımcı olacak bir karakter seçebilirsin. Karakterin stilini kendin seçebilirsin!
