context:
Surface: Settings → App style screen — toggle / row title that switches the Home screen mascot fill style between Classic (monochrome silhouette) and Colored (full multi-color artwork). Adjacent segmented control values are characterFillStyleClassic / characterFillStyleColored.
  Type: setting row title.
  Context: Names the setting that controls whether the mascot character on the Home screen fills with its true colors (Colored) or as a single-color silhouette (Classic) as hydration progress rises.
  Constraints: Keep short for a settings row title; sentence case.

t.ja: キャラクターをカラーで塗る

context:
Surface: app-wide — volume unit suffix on main / widget / watch / Siri / notification surfaces (US / imperial units).
  Type: unit abbreviation.
  Context: Fluid-ounce abbreviation, shown after a numeric volume value (e.g. "8 fl oz"). US / imperial volume unit, the imperial counterpart of `ml` / `l`. Note this is fluid-ounce (volume), not weight-ounce (`oz`).
  Placeholders:
    |R| — required-locale marker (not shown to user) — preserve at the start of the value when translating.
  Constraints: Keep short — typically ≤6 chars; use the standard abbreviation in the target language, never expand to the full phrase ("fluid ounces").

Surface: Friend endorsements (kudos) emoji picker — VoiceOver label for the water-drop emoji button. One of the three named preset emojis (wave / smile / drop); siblings: friendEndorsementWaveA11y, friendEndorsementSmileA11y.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken description of the water-drop emoji used as an endorsement reaction. Translate the noun phrase naturally for spoken form, keeping "emoji" or its locale equivalent.
  Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken noun phrase; preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

t.vi: Giọt nước, biểu tượng cảm xúc

context:
Surface: Friend endorsements (kudos) emoji picker — VoiceOver label for the "more emoji" / "open full emoji picker" button shown after the preset emojis.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken description for the button that lets the user pick a custom emoji (not in the preset set) to send as an endorsement reaction. Imperative-feeling phrase suitable for spoken form.
  Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken action description; preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

t.vi: Chọn biểu tượng cảm xúc khác

context:
Surface: Main screen "Received endorsements" (kudos) cell and friends item cells — VoiceOver accessibility hint announced when the user can tap to open a friend's profile from a received-endorsement entry.
  Type: accessibility hint.
  Context: Spoken hint for the tap-to-open-profile action. Inserts the friend's display name as a possessive ("Open %@ profile" → "Open Alex profile" / "Open Alex's profile" depending on locale grammar). Translate so the possessive reads naturally in target language.
  Placeholders:
    %@ — friend's display name (login or full name), e.g. "Alex"
  Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken phrase; preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Main screen "Received endorsements" cell and friends item cells — VoiceOver fragment describing a single received endorsement (one emoji reaction from one friend). Multiple fragments are joined together for the cell's full accessibility label.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken phrase like "smile from Alex" / "wave from Maria" — names the emoji and the sender. Indexed placeholders allow re-ordering in target languages (e.g. some languages may prefer "от Алекса смайл" / sender-first phrasing).
  Placeholders:
    %1$@ — emoji accessibility label (e.g. "smile", "wave", "water drop")
    %2$@ — friend display name (login or full name)
  Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural; preserve the indexed placeholder syntax (%1$@ / %2$@) — translator may swap their order; preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Friends item cells — VoiceOver label announced for the received-endorsements icon group on a friend row (shown when the friend has sent the current user emoji reactions today).
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken summary identifying that the icons represent emoji reactions received from a friend (general descriptor; the specific emojis are announced separately via friendEndorsementReceivedA11y).
  Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken noun phrase; preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

t.ja: 友達からの絵文字リアクション
t.vi: Biểu tượng cảm xúc từ bạn bè

context:
Surface: Friends item cell, friend profile statistics cell — VoiceOver label for the action button that withdraws an emoji endorsement the current user already sent to this friend today (tapping the button removes the previously-sent emoji).
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken action description like "Remove sent smile" / "Remove sent wave" — communicates that tapping un-sends the named emoji.
  Placeholders:
    %@ — the emoji's raw character/glyph (e.g. "👋", "🙂", "💧"); VoiceOver reads the emoji as its own spoken name when announcing
  Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken action; preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Friend profile statistics cell — VoiceOver label for the badge button that opens the emoji picker to send an endorsement, when no recipient name is available (generic variant; sibling friendEndorsementSendToA11y is used when the friend's name is known).
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken action description for the generic "send emoji endorsement" entry point. Imperative verb phrase.
  Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken action; preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Friends item cells — VoiceOver label for the badge button that opens the emoji picker to send an endorsement to a specific friend (name-aware variant; sibling friendEndorsementSendEmojiA11y is the generic variant without a name).
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken action description like "Send emoji to Alex". Inserts the recipient's display name.
  Placeholders:
    %@ — recipient friend's display name (login or full name), e.g. "Alex"
  Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken action; preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

t.vi: Gửi biểu tượng cảm xúc cho [%s]

context:
Surface: Friend endorsements emoji picker — accessibility label for a non-preset (custom / freshly typed) emoji button.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Read aloud by VoiceOver when the selected endorsement emoji is not one of the named presets (wave / smile / drop); names the emoji character itself instead of a preset label.
  Placeholders:
    %@ — the actual emoji character (e.g. "🎉")
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Friend endorsements emoji picker — accessibility label for the "smile" preset emoji (😊).
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken by VoiceOver on the smile emoji button in the row of preset endorsement emojis sent to friends. Names the emoji so blind users can pick a specific reaction.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

t.vi: Mặt cười, biểu tượng cảm xúc

context:
Surface: Friend endorsements emoji picker — accessibility label for the "wave" preset emoji (👋).
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken by VoiceOver on the wave emoji button in the row of preset endorsement emojis sent to friends. Names the emoji so blind users can pick a specific reaction.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

t.vi: Bàn tay vẫy, biểu tượng cảm xúc

context:
Surface: Main screen "Received endorsements" (kudos) cell, accessibility label summarising today's emoji reactions from friends.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken on the kudos cell so VoiceOver users hear the joined summary of emojis received today.
  Placeholders:
    %@ — comma-joined summary of received emojis and counts assembled in code (e.g. "smile, wave, 2 more kudos")
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Main screen "Received endorsements" (kudos) cell, visible title.
  Type: section header.
  Context: Visible heading on the kudos pill / cell shown on the main screen above today's received emoji reactions from friends.
  Constraints: Keep short (fits the kudos pill on one line).

Surface: app-wide — abbreviated unit suffix appended after sugar / caffeine numeric values (main screen sugar / caffeine cards, statistics charts, beverage details, drink creator preview, etc.).

context:
Surface: Create / edit drink screen — value displayed in the sugar field (`CreateDrinkViewController.set(freeSugarGramsPer100Ml:)`) and read aloud as the accessibility value for sugar in `DrinksParametersViewModel`.
  Type: unit-formatted value.
  Context: Renders the per-beverage free-sugar value as grams per 100 ml of the beverage (the reference volume the app uses for sugar concentration). Compact form: "<value> g/100 ml".
  Placeholders:
    %@ — sugar amount as a formatted number with 1 decimal (e.g. "5.4", "0.0"); produced by `WaterNumberFormatter`.
  Constraints: Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove). Keep the "g/100 ml" units in the form your locale uses for sugar concentration on nutrition labels; the "/" separator is a per-volume marker, not a fraction. Keep compact — fits next to the sugar field.

Surface: Beverage detail statistics — highlight card title for the "Average" stat (BeverageDetailStatisticViewController).
  Type: section header / stat card title.
  Context: Title of the highlight card showing the user's average per-day amount of this beverage for the selected period. Single-word noun.
  Constraints: Keep very short (≤ ~12 chars) — fits a stat card title; preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Beverage detail statistics — highlight card title for the "Best day" stat (BeverageDetailStatisticViewController).
  Type: section header / stat card title.
  Context: Title of the highlight card showing the day with the highest logged amount of this beverage during the selected period.
  Constraints: Keep very short (≤ ~12 chars) — fits a stat card title; preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Beverage detail statistics — chart section title and chart accessibility label (BeverageDetailStatisticViewController).
  Type: section header / accessibility label.
  Context: Title of the per-beverage chart card on the Beverage detail statistics screen. Used both as the visible section header and as the chart view's `accessibilityLabel` for VoiceOver.
  Constraints: Keep short (≤ ~25 chars) — fits a chart card header; preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Beverage detail statistics — VoiceOver accessibility label for the chart when comparison mode is enabled (BeverageDetailStatisticViewController).
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: VoiceOver-only label that announces the chart together with the comparison period (e.g. "Beverage chart, comparison vs Last week"). Placeholder order must be flexible — translator may reorder `%1$@` and `%2$@` for natural language flow.
  Placeholders:
    %1$@ — the chart title (currently localized as "Beverage chart" from key `statisticsBeverageChart`)
    %2$@ — the comparison period label (e.g. "Last week", "Last month")
  Constraints: VoiceOver only — no character limit, but keep it one natural-sounding sentence. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove). Use the indexed placeholders so target languages can reorder.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → chart header, label for the comparison-period column (paired with `statisticsBeverageCurrentPeriod`).
  Type: section header / label.
  Context: Short header above the previous-period total in the two-column chart header (e.g. "vs last" / "This" pair). Followed visually by the period name ("week", "month") rendered separately. Sentence-style casing.
  Constraints: Keep very short (1-2 words) — sits next to a number in a narrow header column. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → chart header, label for the current-period column (paired with `statisticsBeverageComparisonPeriod`).
  Type: section header / label.
  Context: Short header above the current-period total in the two-column chart header (e.g. "This" / "vs last" pair). Followed visually by the period name ("week", "month") rendered separately. Sentence-style casing.
  Constraints: Keep very short (1 word if possible) — sits next to a number in a narrow header column. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → Highlights grid, tile title for the entries-count metric.
  Type: section header / metric label.
  Context: Title above the number of individual drink entries logged in the selected period (e.g. "Entries" with a count below). Noun.
  Constraints: Keep short (1-2 words) to fit a compact highlights tile. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "Highlights & insights" section header.
  Type: section header.
  Context: Header above the highlights grid (best day, average, entries count, water balance) and the tip cards. Plain noun phrase, sentence case.

context:
Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → percentage-of-total label inside the chart / breakdown row.
  Type: paragraph (inline metric).
  Context: Shows a beverage's volume alongside its share of the period's total intake (e.g. "Water 350 ml: 42% of total"). Placeholder order may be reordered by the translator if natural language flow requires it.
  Placeholders:
    %1$@ — formatted volume with unit (e.g. "350 ml", "1.2 l", "12 fl oz")
    %2$d — integer percentage (e.g. 42), immediately followed by the universal literal-percent token [%] so it reads "42%"
  Constraints: Preserve the |R| prefix. Keep [%] right after the percentage number (renders as a single "42%" token).

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → previous-period label when the selected period is "week".
  Type: label.
  Context: Used as the comparison-period name (e.g. shown next to the previous-week total, or interpolated into tip cards). Sentence case noun phrase.
  Constraints: Keep short (1-2 words). Preserve the |R| prefix.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "When you drink" time-of-day chart, bucket label.
  Type: label (chart bucket / axis label).
  Context: Name of the daytime bucket (roughly midday hours). Sibling of `statisticsBeverageTimeMorning` / `statisticsBeverageTimeEvening` / `statisticsBeverageTimeNight`. Also lowercased and reused inside tip cards (`statisticsBeverageTipLowPeriod` / `…Reminder`), so the noun must read naturally lowercased mid-sentence too.
  Constraints: Single word; keep short to fit a narrow chart bucket label. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "When you drink" time-of-day chart, bucket label.
  Type: label (chart bucket / axis label).
  Context: Name of the evening bucket. Sibling of `statisticsBeverageTimeMorning` / `statisticsBeverageTimeDay` / `statisticsBeverageTimeNight`. Also lowercased and reused inside tip cards (`statisticsBeverageTipLowPeriod` / `…Reminder`), so the noun must read naturally lowercased mid-sentence too.
  Constraints: Single word; keep short to fit a narrow chart bucket label. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "When you drink" time-of-day chart, bucket label.
  Type: label (chart bucket / axis label).
  Context: Name of the morning bucket. Sibling of `statisticsBeverageTimeDay` / `statisticsBeverageTimeEvening` / `statisticsBeverageTimeNight`. Also lowercased and reused inside tip cards (`statisticsBeverageTipLowPeriod` / `…Reminder`), so the noun must read naturally lowercased mid-sentence too.
  Constraints: Single word; keep short to fit a narrow chart bucket label. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "When you drink" time-of-day chart, bucket label.
  Type: label (chart bucket / axis label).
  Context: Name of the night bucket. Sibling of `statisticsBeverageTimeMorning` / `statisticsBeverageTimeDay` / `statisticsBeverageTimeEvening`. Lowercased version may also appear in tips, though the night bucket is currently excluded from the low-period reminder tip.
  Constraints: Single word; keep short to fit a narrow chart bucket label. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "When you drink" section subtitle, just below `statisticsBeverageTimeOfDayTitle`.
  Type: subtitle / paragraph.
  Context: Explains that the time-of-day chart shows each bucket's share of the day's intake as a percentage. The value intentionally starts with a literal "%" sign — keep that "%" character as-is, do not convert it to a format specifier.
  Constraints: Keep short (single line under the section title). Preserve the leading "%" literal and the |R| prefix.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "When you drink" section title (above the time-of-day breakdown chart).
  Type: section header.
  Context: Header for the chart that breaks the user's drinking down by time-of-day bucket (morning / day / evening / night).
  Constraints: Keep concise — single section header line. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → first tip card, "behind" variant — the emphasized volume fragment that gets embedded into `statisticsBeverageTipDown`.
  Type: tip (fragment).
  Context: Short phrase meaning "<amount> behind the previous period's pace", used as the visually emphasized substring inside the down-trend tip. Example assembled sentence: "You're 200 ml behind last week's pace.".
  Placeholders:
    %@ — formatted volume with unit (e.g. "200 ml", "0.2 l", "7 fl oz")
  Constraints: Keep short — this fragment must read naturally when interpolated into `statisticsBeverageTipDown`. Preserve the |R| prefix.
  Tone: encouraging — observational, not blaming.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → first tip card, shown when the user drank less than in the previous comparable period.
  Type: tip.
  Context: Negative-trend tip sentence; the first placeholder receives the already-assembled `statisticsBeverageTipBehind` fragment (e.g. "200 ml behind") and the second is the localized period noun supplied by the app, lowercased (e.g. "week", "month"). Translator may reorder the placeholders so the sentence reads naturally in the target language.
  Placeholders:
    %1$@ (first %@) — emphasized fragment from `statisticsBeverageTipBehind` (e.g. "200 ml behind")
    %2$@ (second %@) — period name (e.g. "week", "month", "year", "day")
  Constraints: Preserve the |R| prefix. Keep the trailing period. Both placeholders are non-indexed `%@` in source — if the target language needs reordering, switch to `%1$@` / `%2$@` form.
  Tone: encouraging — observational, supportive (not blaming the user for falling behind).

context:
Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → header eyebrow above the period total.
  Type: section header (eyebrow / overline).
  Context: Small "Total" label rendered above the total volume number for the selected period. Noun.
  Constraints: Keep very short (1 word). Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Onboarding paywall — Statistics preview card, third sample row date label (paired with "Today" and "Yesterday" rows above it).
  Type: sample date label (mock data).
  Context: Hardcoded sample calendar date shown inside a fake statistics card on the paywall to illustrate what the in-app history will look like. Not a live date. Use the translator's locale-natural format for "21 April" (day + month, no year, short month name allowed); the card is narrow so keep it brief.
  Constraints: Keep short (≤10 chars) — the sample row is narrow. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

Surface: Settings → App Style — row title for the "Volume display" option and the title of the resulting picker screen.
  Type: settings row title / screen title.
  Context: Lets the user pick how drink volumes are displayed throughout the app (e.g. raw ml/oz vs. glasses / cups). Noun phrase.
  Constraints: Keep short to fit a settings row title.

Surface: Main / widget / watch hydration progress text — "drunk so far" amount renderer (`WaterAmountDisplayFormat.string(currentMl:goalMl:units:)`, `.drunk` case). Compare `.outOf` (current of goal) and `waterAmountRemaining` (`.remaining`).
  Type: paragraph (progress summary).
  Context: Short status sentence shown next to / under the hydration progress bar, communicating how much water the user has consumed so far today. The English source uses past-participle "drunk" followed by the amount.
  Placeholders:
    %@ — formatted volume with units (e.g. "1.5 L", "1500 ml", "12 fl oz"); composed at runtime from the user's preferred units. Reorder relative to the verb if your locale puts the verb after the amount.
  Constraints: Lowercase as in source (sits as a short label, not a sentence header); keep concise to fit progress text surfaces.

Surface: Main / widget / watch hydration progress text — "remaining to goal" amount renderer (`WaterAmountDisplayFormat.string(currentMl:goalMl:units:)`, `.remaining` case). Compare `.outOf` (current of goal) and `waterAmountDrunk` (`.drunk`).
  Type: paragraph (progress summary).
  Context: Short status phrase shown next to / under the hydration progress bar, telling the user how much water is still needed to reach today's goal. The English source uses "<amount> left" — amount first, "left" after.
  Placeholders:
    %@ — formatted volume with units (e.g. "0.5 L", "500 ml", "5 fl oz"); composed at runtime from the user's preferred units. Reorder relative to "left" if your locale puts the qualifier before the amount.
  Constraints: Lowercase as in source (short progress label, not a sentence header); keep concise to fit progress text surfaces.

Surface: Settings → App Style → Tabs section — title of the toggle row that adds / removes the Weight tracker tab; also the navigation title of the Weight tracker settings screen.
  Type: settings row title / screen title.
  Context: Names the weight tracking feature as a toggleable section the user can show or hide. "Tracker" framing matches how the app refers to its tracking surfaces. The "|R|" prefix is a translation-management marker — keep as the literal first 3 characters of the value; it is stripped at runtime by the app.
  Constraints: Keep "|R| " prefix (with trailing space) exactly as-is. Keep the visible part short to fit a settings row title.

Surface: Apple Watch app — Daily drinks list, empty-state message shown when no drinks have been logged for the day yet.
  Type: empty state.
  Context: Tells the user that no drinks have been logged for the current day on the Watch companion. Below it sits the Add button.
  Constraints: Short — Watch screens are narrow; allow wrapping to multiple lines.

context:

  Placeholders:

  Constraints: Watch-narrow — keep to 1-2 short lines, no trailing period. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

    %@ — formatted recurring price after the trial ends (e.g. "$29.99 / year")
  Constraints: Watch-narrow — keep concise; comma after "free" reads naturally on one line. Preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

t.vi: Dùng thử miễn phí, sau đó [%s]

context: MARK: - Accessibility (VoiceOver)

Surface: Statistics screens — main monthly stat cell (previous-month arrow), beverage detail statistic period switch, weight period switch.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken on the left arrow button that navigates to the previous time period (month / range) in period-switcher controls.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Statistics and Weight screens, forward chevron that advances the visible period (week / month).
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: VoiceOver reads this on the right arrow that moves the period picker to the next interval.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Tips story-format viewer, back button.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken on the back button that navigates to the previous story in a sequential tips story playback.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Tips stories player, forward navigation button.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: VoiceOver reads this on the control that advances the tip story carousel to the next story.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Reorderable lists (drinks parameters, favorite beverages, default volumes), VoiceOver custom action.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken as the name of the rotor / custom action that moves the focused row one position up the list.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Reorderable lists (drinks parameters, favorite beverages, default volumes), VoiceOver custom action.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken as the name of the rotor / custom action that moves the focused row one position down the list.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Beverage chooser cells and beverage cards across the app — VoiceOver value for premium-gated drinks (Choose Beverage, Favorite drink cell, Create-own-drink cell, Edit added drink, Siri DrinkEntity, Spotlight indexer).
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken as the VoiceOver value for a beverage that is locked behind My Water Premium, conveying both the premium gating and the locked state in one phrase.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: app-wide, accessibility value for a non-tappable / disabled option (e.g. a calendar day after today, a locked control).
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken as the VoiceOver value (state indicator) on a row or control that cannot be activated right now.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Statistics calendar day cell, VoiceOver announcement for dates after today.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken when VoiceOver focuses a non-tappable future date cell to explain it cannot be selected yet.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Custom volume entry keypad (watchOS), clear / backspace key.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken on the keypad clear button that removes the entered amount.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Custom volume entry keypad (watchOS), decimal "." key.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken on the keypad decimal-point button used to enter fractional amounts.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Custom volume entry keypad (watchOS), confirm / save key.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken on the keypad confirm button that saves the entered drink amount and dismisses the keypad.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Account editing screen, user avatar button.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken on the avatar button that opens the photo picker to update the profile photo.
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

t.ja: プロフィール写真を変更

context:
Surface: Share screen, accessibility label on each share-target button (Instagram, Facebook, Messages, etc.).
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken on a share button so VoiceOver users know which destination the button shares to.
  Placeholders:
    %@ — destination name (e.g. "Instagram", "Facebook", "Messages")
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Main screen progress pill, accessibility value read after the "Daily hydration" label.
  Type: accessibility label.
  Context: Spoken progress summary value for the main progress pill. The first placeholder is the app-style-specific displayed hydration text; the second is the percent string.
  Placeholders:
    %1$@ — displayed hydration progress text from `WaterAmountDisplayFormat` (e.g. "1.2 l of 2 l", "drunk 1.2 l", "500 ml left")
    %2$@ — percent string (e.g. "40%")
  Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment. Indexed placeholders — preserve %1$@ / %2$@ tokens; order may be changed if target grammar requires it.

  Context: Adjective + noun. Label for the option that lets the user pick a custom reminder interval (instead of the preset choices). Tapping it opens a wheel duration picker. Distinct from "customPeriod" / "customRange" used for statistics date ranges.
  Constraints: Keep short to fit a popup row / navigation bar title. No trailing period.

Surface: Onboarding paywall — hero title at the top of the subscribe screen, first (top) line.
  Type: hero title — first line of a composed two-line headline.
  Context: Opening half of the onboarding paywall headline. Concatenated visually with "onboardingSubscribeTitleHighlightedLine" on the line below to form a single sentence "Start drinking more water / with My Water app", where the second line is rendered with a highlight underline behind it. Translate as the first half of one continuous sentence — keep the natural pause / word order so the highlighted second line reads as the emphasised tail of the same phrase. Also concatenated into the VoiceOver label for the whole title.
  Constraints: 1 short line; auto-shrinks but truncates on long phrasing. Avoid trailing punctuation — the sentence continues on the next line.

t.ar: ابدأ في شرب المزيد من الماء
t.ja: もっと水を飲み始めましょう

context:
Surface: Onboarding paywall — hero title at the top of the subscribe screen, second (bottom) line rendered with a hand-drawn highlight stroke behind the text.
  Type: hero title — emphasised second line of a composed two-line headline.
  Context: Closing half of the onboarding paywall headline; pairs with "onboardingSubscribeTitleFirstLine" above to form "Start drinking more water / with My Water app". This line is the emphasised tail of the sentence — keep the brand name "My Water" exactly as written (do not translate or transliterate the product name). Also concatenated into the VoiceOver label for the whole title.
  Constraints: 1 short line; auto-shrinks but truncates on long phrasing. Must read naturally as the continuation of the first line.

en: Our advertising partners may use calendar access to help deliver relevant ads and measure ad performance.
context: Required because an advertising SDK may request calendar access; the app does not use Calendar directly.

t.ar: قد يستخدم شركاؤنا الإعلانيون الوصول إلى التقويم للمساعدة في عرض إعلانات مناسبة وقياس أداء الإعلانات.
t.da: Vores annoncepartnere kan bruge kalenderadgang til at vise relevante annoncer og måle annoncernes effekt.
t.de: Unsere Werbepartner können den Kalenderzugriff nutzen, um relevante Anzeigen auszuliefern und deren Leistung zu messen.
t.en: Our advertising partners may use calendar access to help deliver relevant ads and measure ad performance.
t.es: Nuestros socios publicitarios pueden usar el acceso al calendario para mostrar anuncios relevantes y medir su rendimiento.
t.fr: Nos partenaires publicitaires peuvent utiliser l'accès au calendrier pour diffuser des publicités pertinentes et en mesurer les performances.
t.hi: हमारे विज्ञापन साझेदार प्रासंगिक विज्ञापन दिखाने और उनके प्रदर्शन को मापने के लिए कैलेंडर एक्सेस का उपयोग कर सकते हैं।

t.it: I nostri partner pubblicitari possono usare l'accesso al calendario per mostrare annunci pertinenti e misurarne le prestazioni.
t.ja: 広告パートナーが関連性の高い広告の配信と効果測定のためにカレンダーへのアクセスを使用する場合があります。
t.ko: 광고 파트너가 관련성 높은 광고 게재 및 성과 측정을 위해 캘린더 접근 권한을 사용할 수 있습니다.

t.pt_BR: Nossos parceiros de publicidade podem usar o acesso ao calendário para exibir anúncios relevantes e medir o desempenho deles.
t.ru: Наши рекламные партнёры могут использовать доступ к календарю для показа релевантной рекламы и оценки её эффективности.
t.sv: Våra annonspartner kan använda kalenderåtkomst för att visa relevanta annonser och mäta deras effekt.
t.tr: Reklam ortaklarımız ilgili reklamları göstermek ve performanslarını ölçmek için takvim erişimini kullanabilir.
t.vi: Đối tác quảng cáo của chúng tôi có thể dùng quyền truy cập lịch để hiển thị quảng cáo phù hợp và đo lường hiệu quả.

en: My Water uses device motion to animate the water wave on the main screen.

t.ar: يستخدم My Water بيانات حركة الجهاز لتحريك موجة الماء على الشاشة الرئيسية.
t.da: My Water bruger enhedens bevægelse til at animere vandbølgen på hovedskærmen.
t.de: My Water nutzt die Gerätebewegung, um die Wasserwelle auf dem Hauptbildschirm zu animieren.
t.en: My Water uses device motion to animate the water wave on the main screen.
t.es: My Water usa el movimiento del dispositivo para animar la ola de agua en la pantalla principal.
t.fr: My Water utilise le mouvement de l'appareil pour animer la vague d'eau sur l'écran principal.
t.hi: My Water मुख्य स्क्रीन पर पानी की लहर को एनिमेट करने के लिए डिवाइस की गति का उपयोग करता है।
t.id: My Water menggunakan gerakan perangkat untuk menganimasikan gelombang air di layar utama.
t.it: My Water usa il movimento del dispositivo per animare l'onda d'acqua nella schermata principale.
t.ja: My Water はデバイスのモーションを使ってメイン画面の水の波をアニメーション表示します。
t.ko: My Water는 기기 모션을 사용해 메인 화면의 물결을 애니메이션으로 표시합니다.
t.ms: My Water menggunakan gerakan peranti untuk menganimasikan gelombang air pada skrin utama.
t.nb: My Water bruker enhetens bevegelse for å animere vannbølgen på hovedskjermen.
t.nl: My Water gebruikt apparaatbeweging om de watergolf op het hoofdscherm te animeren.
t.pl: My Water używa ruchu urządzenia do animowania fali wody na ekranie głównym.
t.pt_BR: O My Water usa o movimento do dispositivo para animar a onda de água na tela principal.
t.ru: Приложение использует данные о движении устройства, чтобы анимировать волну воды на главном экране.
t.sv: My Water använder enhetens rörelse för att animera vattenvågen på huvudskärmen.
t.tr: My Water, ana ekrandaki su dalgasını canlandırmak için cihaz hareketini kullanır.
t.vi: My Water dùng chuyển động của thiết bị để tạo hiệu ứng sóng nước trên màn hình chính.
t.zh_CN: My Water 使用设备运动数据在主屏幕上为水波添加动画效果。

en: We need permission to save images from My Water to your photo library.

t.ar: نحتاج إلى إذن لحفظ الصور من My Water في مكتبة صورك.
t.da: Vi har brug for tilladelse til at gemme billeder fra My Water i dit fotobibliotek.
t.de: Wir benötigen die Berechtigung, Bilder aus My Water in Ihrer Fotomediathek zu speichern.
t.en: We need permission to save images from My Water to your photo library.
t.es: Necesitamos permiso para guardar imágenes de My Water en tu fototeca.
t.fr: Nous avons besoin de l'autorisation d'enregistrer des images de My Water dans votre photothèque.
t.hi: हमें My Water की छवियाँ आपकी फ़ोटो लाइब्रेरी में सहेजने की अनुमति चाहिए।
t.id: Kami memerlukan izin untuk menyimpan gambar dari My Water ke pustaka foto Anda.
t.it: Ci serve l'autorizzazione per salvare le immagini di My Water nella tua libreria foto.
t.ja: My Water の画像を写真ライブラリに保存するために許可が必要です。
t.ko: My Water의 이미지를 사진 보관함에 저장하려면 권한이 필요합니다.
t.ms: Kami memerlukan kebenaran untuk menyimpan imej daripada My Water ke pustaka foto anda.
t.nb: Vi trenger tillatelse til å lagre bilder fra My Water i fotobiblioteket ditt.
t.nl: We hebben toestemming nodig om afbeeldingen van My Water in je fotobibliotheek op te slaan.
t.pl: Potrzebujemy pozwolenia na zapisywanie obrazów z My Water w Twojej bibliotece zdjęć.
t.pt_BR: Precisamos de permissão para salvar imagens do My Water na sua fototeca.
t.ru: Нам нужно разрешение, чтобы сохранять изображения из My Water в вашу медиатеку.
t.sv: Vi behöver behörighet att spara bilder från My Water i ditt fotobibliotek.
t.tr: My Water'daki görselleri fotoğraf kitaplığınıza kaydetmek için izninize ihtiyacımız var.
t.vi: Vui lòng cho phép chúng tôi lưu hình ảnh từ My Water vào thư viện ảnh của bạn.
t.zh_CN: 请授予我们将 My Water 中的图片保存到您的照片图库的权限。

t.sv: Våra annonspartner kan använda Bluetooth-signaler för att visa relevanta annonser och mäta deras effekt.
t.tr: Reklam ortaklarımız ilgili reklamları göstermek ve performanslarını ölçmek için Bluetooth sinyallerini kullanabilir.
t.vi: Đối tác quảng cáo của chúng tôi có thể dùng tín hiệu Bluetooth để hiển thị quảng cáo phù hợp và đo lường hiệu quả.
t.zh_CN: 我们的广告合作伙伴可能会使用蓝牙信号来投放相关广告并衡量广告效果。

en: Could not process this photo. Please choose another image.
context:
Surface: Account editing screen / avatar flow.
Type: Short error message.
Context: Shown when the selected or captured image could not be decoded, loaded, or cropped.
Covers: Unsupported image format, corrupt file, cloud-only image unavailable locally, or cropper failure.
Meaning: “Photo” refers to the avatar/profile image.
User action: The user should choose another image.
Constraints: No placeholders. No pluralization. Keep concise.

t.en: Could not process this photo. Please choose another image.

en: Could not save the photo. Please try again.
context:
Surface: Account editing screen / avatar flow.
Type: Short error message.
Context: Shown after returning from the camera when the app cannot recover or read the captured temporary image.
Important: This happens before cropping and before uploading.
Meaning: The local captured photo file could not be saved, recovered, or opened by the app.
User action: The user should try taking the photo again.
Constraints: No placeholders. No pluralization. Keep concise.

t.en: Could not save the photo. Please try again.

en: Could not open the camera. Please try again.
context:
Surface: Account editing screen / avatar flow.
Type: Short error message.
Context: Shown when the app failed to open the camera capture UI.
Covers: Missing camera app, unavailable hardware, or camera launch/FileProvider failure.
Important: Do not phrase this as a permission-only error.
User action: The user can retry or choose another image source.
Constraints: No placeholders. No pluralization. Keep concise.

t.en: Could not open the camera. Please try again.

en: Could not open the photo picker. Please try again.
context:
Surface: Account editing screen / avatar flow.
Type: Short error message.
Context: Shown when the app failed to open the system photo picker or document picker UI.
Important: This is not a photo-library permission denial.
Do not translate it as “allow photo access”.
User action: The user can retry or choose another image source.
Constraints: No placeholders. No pluralization. Keep concise.

t.en: Could not open the photo picker. Please try again.
t.ru: Не удалось открыть выбор фото. Пожалуйста, попробуйте снова.

context:
Surface: Settings screen — row title for the cross-promotion entry point that opens the studio's other apps (App Store listing / promo screen).
  Type: settings row title.
  Context: Header / row title introducing the studio's other apps as a cross-promotion. "Our" = the studio's, framing the studio as a partner ("we" / "our app"). Pairs with installOtherLabel on the destination promo screen.
  Constraints: Keep short to fit a settings row.

t.vi: Những ứng dụng khác của chúng tôi

en: Fitbit did not grant nutrition access. Reconnect Fitbit and allow nutrition permission.
context:
Section: AI-translated backlog keys added 2026-05-22 for hi.lproj.
  EN source keeps |R| markers until human/Lokalise review.

t.ar: لم يمنح Fitbit صلاحية الوصول إلى التغذية. أعد ربط Fitbit واسمح بصلاحية التغذية
t.da: Fitbit gav ikke adgang til ernæring. Tilslut Fitbit igen, og giv tilladelse til ernæring.
t.de: Fitbit hat keinen Zugriff auf Ernährungsdaten gewährt. Verbinden Sie Fitbit erneut und erlauben Sie die Ernährungsberechtigung.
t.en: Fitbit did not grant nutrition access. Reconnect Fitbit and allow nutrition permission.
t.es: Fitbit no concedió acceso a nutrición. Vuelve a conectar Fitbit y permite el permiso de nutrición.
t.fr: Fitbit n'a pas accordé l'accès à la nutrition. Reconnecte Fitbit et autorise la permission nutrition.
t.hi: Fitbit ने न्यूट्रिशन एक्सेस नहीं दिया। Fitbit को फिर से कनेक्ट करें और न्यूट्रिशन परमिशन दें।
t.id: Fitbit tidak memberikan akses nutrisi. Hubungkan kembali Fitbit dan izinkan akses nutrisi.
t.it: Fitbit non ha concesso l'accesso alla nutrizione. Riconnetti Fitbit e consenti l'autorizzazione per la nutrizione.
t.ja: Fitbitから栄養情報へのアクセスが許可されませんでした。Fitbitを再接続して栄養情報の権限を許可してください。
t.ko: Fitbit이 영양 정보 접근 권한을 부여하지 않았어요. Fitbit을 다시 연결하고 영양 정보 권한을 허용해 주세요.
t.ms: Fitbit tidak memberikan akses pemakanan. Sambung semula Fitbit dan benarkan kebenaran pemakanan.
t.nb: Fitbit ga ikke tilgang til ernæring. Koble til Fitbit på nytt og tillat ernæringstilgang
t.nl: Fitbit verleende geen toegang tot voeding. Verbind Fitbit opnieuw en sta de toestemming voor voeding toe.
t.pl: Fitbit nie udzielił dostępu do informacji o odżywianiu. Połącz Fitbit ponownie i zezwól na uprawnienie do informacji o odżywianiu.
t.pt_BR: O Fitbit não concedeu acesso à nutrição. Reconecte o Fitbit e permita o acesso à nutrição.
t.ru: Fitbit не предоставил доступ к данным о питании. Переподключите Fitbit и разрешите доступ к питанию.
t.sv: Fitbit beviljade inte tillgång till nutrition. Återanslut Fitbit och tillåt behörighet för nutrition.
t.tr: Fitbit beslenme erişimi vermedi. Fitbit'i yeniden bağla ve beslenme iznini ver.
t.vi: Fitbit không cấp quyền truy cập dinh dưỡng. Hãy kết nối lại Fitbit và cho phép quyền dinh dưỡng
t.zh_CN: Fitbit 未授予营养数据权限。请重新连接 Fitbit 并允许营养数据访问。

en: Could not disconnect Fitbit. Check your connection and try again.
context:
Surface: Settings → Integrations → Fitbit disconnect alert — shown when the app cannot revoke Fitbit OAuth tokens while disconnecting.
  Type: alert body.
  Context: The integration remains connected locally because server-side revoke did not succeed.

t.ar: تعذّر فصل Fitbit. تحقق من اتصالك وحاول مرة أخرى
t.da: Kunne ikke afbryde Fitbit. Tjek din forbindelse, og prøv igen.
t.de: Fitbit konnte nicht getrennt werden. Überprüfen Sie Ihre Verbindung und versuchen Sie es erneut.
t.en: Could not disconnect Fitbit. Check your connection and try again.
t.es: No se pudo desconectar Fitbit. Comprueba tu conexión e inténtalo de nuevo.
t.fr: Impossible de déconnecter Fitbit. Vérifie ta connexion et réessaie.
t.hi: Fitbit डिस्कनेक्ट नहीं हो सका। अपना कनेक्शन जाँचें और फिर से कोशिश करें।
t.id: Tidak dapat memutuskan Fitbit. Periksa koneksi Anda dan coba lagi.
t.it: Impossibile disconnettere Fitbit. Controlla la connessione e riprova.
t.ja: Fitbitを切断できませんでした。接続を確認してもう一度お試しください。
t.ko: Fitbit 연결을 해제할 수 없어요. 연결 상태를 확인하고 다시 시도해 주세요.
t.ms: Tidak dapat memutuskan sambungan Fitbit. Semak sambungan awak dan cuba lagi.
t.nb: Kunne ikke koble fra Fitbit. Sjekk tilkoblingen og prøv igjen
t.nl: Kan Fitbit niet ontkoppelen. Controleer je verbinding en probeer het opnieuw.
t.pl: Nie udało się rozłączyć z Fitbit. Sprawdź połączenie i spróbuj ponownie.
t.pt_BR: Não foi possível desconectar o Fitbit. Verifique sua conexão e tente novamente.
t.ru: Не удалось отключить Fitbit. Проверьте подключение и попробуйте снова.
t.sv: Kunde inte koppla från Fitbit. Kontrollera din anslutning och försök igen.
t.tr: Fitbit bağlantısı kesilemedi. Bağlantını kontrol edip tekrar dene.
t.vi: Không thể ngắt kết nối Fitbit. Hãy kiểm tra kết nối và thử lại
t.zh_CN: 无法断开 Fitbit。请检查网络连接后重试。

en: No drinks in this category yet. Drag a drink here to add it
context:
Surface: Drink parameters (Beverages) screen — placeholder shown on the dashed plate inside an empty drink category section (all of its drinks were hidden or dragged into other categories).
  Type: empty state.
  Context: Reassures the user the empty section is intentional, not a bug, and explains how to fill it — by dragging a drink into this category. Shown beneath the category header.
  Constraints: Keep concise to fit two short lines on small iPhones; preserve the |R| prefix (translation-pipeline marker — do not translate or remove).

t.ar: لا توجد مشروبات في هذه الفئة بعد. اسحب مشروبًا إلى هنا لإضافته
t.da: Ingen drikke i denne kategori endnu. Træk en drik herhen for at tilføje den
t.de: In dieser Kategorie sind noch keine Getränke. Ziehe ein Getränk hierher, um es hinzuzufügen
t.en: No drinks in this category yet. Drag a drink here to add it
t.es: Aún no hay bebidas en esta categoría. Arrastra una bebida aquí para añadirla
t.fr: Aucune boisson dans cette catégorie pour le moment. Fais glisser une boisson ici pour l'ajouter
t.hi: इस कैटेगरी में अभी कोई पेय नहीं है। यहाँ कोई पेय खींचकर लाओ
t.id: Belum ada minuman di kategori ini. Seret minuman ke sini untuk menambahkannya
t.it: In questa categoria non ci sono ancora bevande. Trascina qui una bevanda per aggiungerla
t.ja: このカテゴリーにはまだ飲み物がありません。ここに飲み物をドラッグして追加してください
t.ko: 이 카테고리에는 아직 음료가 없어요. 음료를 여기로 드래그해서 추가해 보세요
t.ms: Tiada minuman dalam kategori ini. Seret minuman ke sini untuk menambahnya
t.nb: Ingen drikker i denne kategorien ennå. Dra en drikke hit for å legge til
t.nl: Nog geen drankjes in deze categorie. Sleep een drankje hierheen om het toe te voegen
t.pl: W tej kategorii nie ma jeszcze napojów. Przeciągnij tutaj napój, aby go dodać
t.pt_BR: Ainda não há bebidas nesta categoria. Arraste uma bebida até aqui para adicioná-la
t.ru: В этой категории пока нет напитков. Перетащи сюда напиток, чтобы добавить
t.sv: Inga drycker i den här kategorin än. Dra en dryck hit för att lägga till
t.tr: Bu kategoride henüz içecek yok. Eklemek için buraya bir içecek sürükle
t.vi: Chưa có đồ uống nào trong danh mục này. Hãy kéo một đồ uống vào đây để thêm
t.zh_CN: 此类别还没有饮品。把饮品拖到这里就能添加

context:

  Type: settings picker option label.
  Context: Live Activity continuously visible while hydration reminders are enabled; the app does not auto-dismiss it.

  Type: settings picker option label.
  Context: Live Activity появляется на момент активного напоминания о питье и убирается, когда напоминание уходит.

  Type: settings picker option label.
  Context: Live Activity никогда не появляется.

en: Live Activities are turned off. Please turn them back on in Settings.
context:
Section: AI-translated backlog keys added 2026-05-22 for nl.lproj.
  EN source keeps |R| markers until human/Lokalise review.

t.ar: النشاط المباشر معطّل. يرجى تفعيله من الإعدادات.
t.da: Live Activities er slået fra. Slå dem til igen i Indstillinger.
t.de: Live-Aktivitäten sind ausgeschaltet. Bitte aktivieren Sie sie wieder in den Einstellungen.
t.en: Live Activities are turned off. Please turn them back on in Settings.
t.es: Las Live Activities están desactivadas. Vuelve a activarlas en Ajustes.
t.fr: Les Live Activities sont désactivées. Veuillez les réactiver dans les Réglages.
t.hi: Live Activities बंद हैं। कृपया इन्हें सेटिंग्स में दोबारा चालू करें।
t.id: Live Activity dinonaktifkan. Aktifkan kembali di Pengaturan.
t.it: Le Live Activity sono disattivate. Riattivale nelle Impostazioni.
t.ja: ライブアクティビティがオフになっています。設定からオンに戻してください。
t.ko: 실시간 활동이 꺼져 있어요. 설정에서 다시 켜 주세요.
t.ms: Live Activities dimatikan. Sila hidupkannya semula dalam Tetapan.
t.nb: Live Activities er slått av. Slå dem på igjen i Innstillinger.
t.nl: Live Activities zijn uitgeschakeld. Schakel ze opnieuw in via Instellingen.
t.pl: Live Activities są wyłączone. Włącz je ponownie w Ustawieniach.
t.pt_BR: As Live Activities estão desativadas. Reative-as nas Configurações.
t.ru: Live-активности отключены. Пожалуйста, включите их снова в Настройках.
t.sv: Live Activities är avstängda. Slå på dem igen i Inställningar.
t.tr: Canlı Etkinlikler kapalı. Lütfen Ayarlar'dan yeniden aç.
t.vi: Live Activity đang tắt. Vui lòng bật lại trong Cài đặt.
t.zh_CN: 实时活动已关闭。请在“设置”中重新开启。

  Type: settings section header.
  Context: Names the group of behavioural rules that automatically suppress or continue reminders based on the user's recent activity. Short noun phrase, sentence case.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → Highlights grid, tile title for the most-frequent portion size of this beverage.
  Type: section header / metric label.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → Highlights grid, tile subtitle under the Favorite volume value.
  Type: caption / hint.
  Context: Small caption that clarifies the displayed volume is the user's most frequently chosen single-serving size for this beverage (e.g. "most often" under "250 ml"). Sentence case, no period.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → shared placeholder shown in highlight tiles (Best day), time-of-day cells (Morning/Day/Evening/Night percent and volume), Yesterday/previous-period total card and trend pill when the selected period has no logged drinks of this beverage.
  Type: empty-state placeholder (inline value substitution).
  Context: Replaces an em-dash (—) as a friendlier "nothing to show" stand-in. Used in compact stat tiles and inline pills, so the label must stay very short to avoid truncation. Sentence case, no period.

t.hi: सारा डेटा फिर से सिंक करें
t.vi: Đồng bộ lại tất cả dữ liệu

context:
Surface: Settings → Integrations → Apple Health setup screen — confirmation alert title shown after the user taps the "Re-sync all data" button.
  Type: alert title.
  Context: Asks the user to confirm starting a bulk re-export of all drinks to Apple Health. "Apple Health" is the Apple product name — do not translate.

t.hi: Apple Health के साथ फिर से सिंक करें?
t.ja: Apple Healthに再同期しますか？
t.nb: Synkronisere til Apple Health på nytt?
t.pt_BR: Sincronizar com o Apple Health novamente?
t.tr: Apple Health ile yeniden senkronize edilsin mi?
t.vi: Đồng bộ lại với Apple Health?

en: This will upload every drink from My Water to Apple Health. Duplicates won't be created
context:
Surface: Settings → Integrations → Apple Health setup screen — confirmation alert body shown after the user taps the "Re-sync all data" button.
  Type: alert message / paragraph.
  Context: Reassures the user that this won't create duplicates — the app deduplicates against samples already present in Apple Health. "My Water" and "Apple Health" are product names — do not translate.
  Constraints: ≤ 2 sentences for a system alert.

t.ar: سيتم رفع جميع المشروبات من My Water إلى Apple Health. لن يتم إنشاء نسخ مكررة
t.da: Dette uploader alle drikke fra My Water til Apple Health. Der oprettes ingen dubletter
t.de: Alle Getränke aus My Water werden zu Apple Health hochgeladen. Es entstehen keine Duplikate
t.en: This will upload every drink from My Water to Apple Health. Duplicates won't be created
t.es: Todas las bebidas de My Water se subirán a Apple Health. No se crearán duplicados
t.fr: Toutes les boissons de My Water seront envoyées vers Apple Health. Aucun doublon ne sera créé
t.hi: इससे My Water के सभी पेय Apple Health में अपलोड हो जाएँगे। डुप्लिकेट नहीं बनेंगे
t.id: Ini akan mengunggah setiap minuman dari My Water ke Apple Health. Duplikat tidak akan dibuat
t.it: Tutte le bevande di My Water verranno caricate su Apple Health. Non verranno creati duplicati
t.ja: My Waterのすべての飲み物をApple Healthにアップロードします。重複は作成されません
t.ko: My Water의 모든 음료를 Apple Health에 업로드해요. 중복은 생성되지 않아요
t.ms: Ini akan memuat naik setiap minuman dari My Water ke Apple Health. Pendua tidak akan dicipta
t.nb: Dette vil laste opp alle drikker fra Mitt Vann til Apple Health. Duplikater blir ikke opprettet
t.nl: Hiermee worden alle drankjes uit My Water naar Apple Health geüpload. Er worden geen duplicaten gemaakt
t.pl: Wszystkie napoje z My Water zostaną przesłane do Apple Health. Duplikaty nie zostaną utworzone
t.pt_BR: Isso vai enviar todas as bebidas do My Water para o Apple Health. Não serão criadas duplicatas
t.ru: Все напитки из «Моя вода» будут загружены в Apple Health. Дубликаты не появятся
t.sv: Alla drycker från Mitt Vatten laddas upp till Apple Health. Inga dubbletter skapas
t.tr: Bu işlem My Water'daki tüm içecekleri Apple Health'e yükler. Mükerrer kayıt oluşmaz
t.vi: Tất cả đồ uống từ My Water sẽ được tải lên Apple Health. Sẽ không tạo bản trùng
t.zh_CN: 这会将 My Water 中的所有饮品上传到 Apple Health。不会产生重复记录

context:
Surface: Settings → Integrations → Apple Health setup screen — confirmation alert primary action.
  Type: button label.
  Context: Confirms starting the bulk re-export. Pairs with the generic "cancel" key.
  Constraints: Keep short — alert action.

Surface: Apple Health re-sync drawer modal — large title shown at the top of the modal while the bulk export is in progress.
  Type: drawer title.
  Context: Indicates the running operation to the user. "Apple Health" is the Apple product name — do not translate.
  Constraints: Single line; auto-shrinks.

t.ar: جارٍ إعادة المزامنة مع Apple Health
t.hi: Apple Health के साथ सिंक हो रहा है
t.vi: Đang đồng bộ với Apple Health

en: We're uploading all your drinks. This can take a while. Please keep the app open
context:
Surface: Apple Health re-sync drawer modal — subtitle paragraph below the title, shown while the bulk export is in progress.
  Type: paragraph (informational).
  Context: Tells the user the export may take a while and asks them not to close the app. The export continues in foreground; backgrounding the app risks iOS killing the process and partial state.
  Constraints: 1-2 short sentences.

t.ar: نقوم برفع جميع مشروباتك. قد يستغرق ذلك بعض الوقت. يرجى إبقاء التطبيق مفتوحًا
t.da: Vi uploader alle dine drikke. Det kan tage et stykke tid. Lad venligst appen være åben
t.de: Wir laden alle deine Getränke hoch. Das kann eine Weile dauern. Bitte halte die App geöffnet
t.en: We're uploading all your drinks. This can take a while. Please keep the app open
t.es: Estamos subiendo todas tus bebidas. Esto puede tardar un poco. Mantén la app abierta
t.fr: Envoi de toutes tes boissons en cours. Cela peut prendre un moment. Garde l'application ouverte
t.hi: हम आपके सारे पेय अपलोड कर रहे हैं। इसमें थोड़ा समय लग सकता है। कृपया ऐप खुला रखें
t.id: Kami sedang mengunggah semua minuman Anda. Proses ini mungkin memakan waktu. Mohon biarkan aplikasi terbuka
t.it: Stiamo caricando tutte le tue bevande. Potrebbe richiedere un po' di tempo. Tieni l'app aperta
t.ja: すべての飲み物をアップロードしています。しばらく時間がかかる場合があります。アプリを開いたままにしてください
t.ko: 모든 음료를 업로드하고 있어요. 시간이 좀 걸릴 수 있어요. 앱을 열어 둔 채로 기다려 주세요
t.ms: Kami sedang memuat naik semua minuman awak. Ini mungkin mengambil sedikit masa. Sila pastikan apl ini terbuka
t.nb: Vi laster opp alle drikkene dine. Dette kan ta litt tid. Hold appen åpen
t.nl: We uploaden al je drankjes. Dit kan even duren. Houd de app open
t.pl: Przesyłamy wszystkie twoje napoje. To może chwilę potrwać. Nie zamykaj aplikacji
t.pt_BR: Estamos enviando todas as suas bebidas. Isso pode levar um tempo. Mantenha o aplicativo aberto, por favor
t.ru: Загружаем все напитки. Это может занять время. Пожалуйста, оставьте приложение открытым
t.sv: Vi laddar upp alla dina drycker. Det kan ta en stund. Håll appen öppen
t.tr: Tüm içecekleriniz yükleniyor. Bu biraz sürebilir. Lütfen uygulamayı açık tutun
t.vi: Đang tải lên tất cả đồ uống của bạn. Việc này có thể mất một lúc. Vui lòng giữ ứng dụng mở
t.zh_CN: 正在上传所有饮品。这可能需要一些时间。请保持应用打开

context:
Surface: Apple Health re-sync drawer modal — counter label below the progress bar showing current / total record counts.
  Type: label (numeric counter).
  Context: Updates as each drink is processed. Format is "X of Y" — e.g., "234 of 1024".
  Placeholders:
    %1$d — number of records processed so far
    %2$d — total number of records to process
  Constraints: Keep short. Translation must preserve both ordered placeholders.

en:
New drinks added: [%1$s]
Already in Apple Health: [%2$s]
Records processed: [%3$s]
context:
Surface: Apple Health re-sync drawer modal — success summary shown after the bulk export completes.
  Type: status message (multi-line).
  Context: Splits the result into records newly added to Apple Health, records that were already present, and total records processed so the user doesn't read "new" as the full sync size.
  Placeholders:
    %1$@ — formatted count of newly added drink records
    %2$@ — formatted count of drink records already present in Apple Health
    %3$@ — formatted count of all processed drink records
  Constraints: Keep three short lines. Translation must preserve all ordered placeholders and line breaks.

t.ar:
مشروبات جديدة مضافة: [%1$s]
موجود مسبقًا في Apple Health: [%2$s]
السجلات المعالجة: [%3$s]
t.da:
Nye drikke tilføjet: [%1$s]
Allerede i Apple Health: [%2$s]
Behandlede poster: [%3$s]
t.de:
Neue Getränke hinzugefügt: [%1$s]

en: Couldn't upload: [%1$s]. Please allow all access in Apple Health
